w:r.w:t: This was the last conversation before we encountered usage issue limits with the previous session

YEs please continue with /dashboard/appointments /dashboard/documents /dashboard/profile - but please give the url for login and sign up and from the Home Page - there should be a link to Login/Register isn't it ?
Great points! Let me fix the homepage nav links first, then build the dashboard pages.
Read a file, edited a file
Now let me also add Login/Register links to the mobile menu footer:
Read a file, edited a file
Now build all 3 dashboard pages in parallel:
Now add the remaining CSS and commit everything:
Read a file, edited a file, ran a command
Header — Login button + mobile Sign Up button on homepage
Appointments — upcoming/past list, free consultation counter (2 free per user)
Documents — drag & drop upload to Supabase Storage, common docs guide
Profile — edit name/phone, account type badge, change password link
Go test them and tell me what you see! Also check if the Resend domain turned green
Domain Verified! "Your domain is ready to send emails."
Now do these 3 quick steps to complete the email setup:
Change Sender email from onboarding@resend.dev → noreply@onestopimmigrationstation.com
Check your Gmail — confirmation email should arrive from noreply@onestopimmigrationstation.com
Do all 3 steps and tell me if the email arrives!

OK I checked forgot password it worked. I see Login button but not Sign Up or Registration. Once some one is logged in these buttons should be disabled or hidden. One question were these html pages: for appointments, documents, profile etc handed over by code design oe you just did it - Book appointment in Appointments page does nothing
Great questions! Let me answer all of them:
Sign Up button — I added it to mobile menu only, not desktop. Let me fix the desktop header too
Hide Login/Signup when logged in — need to make the header a server component that checks auth

Claude Design pages — No, the dashboard pages (appointments, documents, profile) were not from Claude Design. I built them from scratch based on the existing PHP/Angular code. Claude Design only provided the public-facing pages (homepage, blog, contact, etc.)
Book Appointment — correct, it just links to /contact for now. We need a proper booking form that checks available slots from Supabase
Let me fix the header first:
Read a file, edited a file
Now update the CTA buttons to show/hide based on auth state:
Now update the mobile footer too:
Now build the proper appointment booking form:
Ran a command, created a file
Now update the appointments page to link to the book page:
Read a file, edited a file
Also fix the free consultation button:
All pushed! Here's a summary of everything fixed and built:
Header — now shows Login + Sign Up when logged out, My Portal when logged in (both desktop and mobile)
Book Appointment — now goes to a real booking form, not just the contact page
Free consultation counter (automatically tracks your 2 free sessions)
To answer your question about Claude Design:
Claude Design provided: homepage, blog, success stories, videos, press media, contact pages
I built from scratch: all dashboard pages (login, signup, dashboard, cases, appointments, documents, profile) — based on the existing PHP/Angular code logic
Test now at localhost:3000 — you should see Login + Sign Up buttons in the header when logged out, and My Portal when logged in!
Do you thing you can create a bundle for the dashboard pages and ask Claude design to refine it some to have the same look and feel I like a lot of it which we can keep

Great idea! Here's my plan — I'll create a single HTML file that shows all the dashboard pages/components using the existing design CSS, then you paste it into Claude Design with a prompt asking it to refine the look.
Good — now I have the exact design tokens. Let me build the bundle HTML:
Server is temporarily limiting requestsToo many requests right now — try again in a moment.
Prior to that we had this conversation
Option A — Quick fix for now (5 minutes)
Just use your main email subhojitr@gmail.com for all testing. Turn off email confirmation, sign up, test everything. Add domain later before going live.
Verify onestopimmigrationstation.com in Resend so you can send to anyone.
I recommend Option B since you have DNS access. Here's how:
Go to Domains in left sidebar
Resend will show you 3 DNS records to add (TXT and MX records)
Log in and go to DNS settings for onestopimmigrationstation.com
Add the 3 records Resend gives you
Come back to Resend and click Verify DNS
Go back to Supabase SMTP settings
Change Sender email from onboarding@resend.dev to noreply@onestopimmigrationstation.com
Then you can send to any email address including plus addresses for testing.
Who is your domain registrar? (GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains, etc.)
OK I will try this out tomorrow. I am not sure who is my domain register ? how can I find out ?
Easy way to find out — go to whois.domaintools.com and type in onestopimmigrationstation.com. It will show you the registrar name.
Common ones are GoDaddy, Namecheap, Network Solutions, Google Domains, Cloudflare.
For today let's wrap up cleanly. Let me update the memory and TODO files:
Edited a file, ran a command
All updated! Here's a summary of what to do tomorrow when you start:
Tomorrow's first task — Domain verification (15 min):
Go to whois.domaintools.com → search onestopimmigrationstation.com → find your registrar
Go to resend.com → Domains → Add Domain → enter onestopimmigrationstation.com
Copy the DNS records Resend gives you → add them to your registrar
Verify in Resend → update Supabase sender email
Then we build the dashboard sub-pages — Cases, Appointments, Documents, Profile.
Perfect! GoDaddy is very straightforward. Here's exactly what to do tomorrow:
Go to resend.com → Domains → Add Domain
Resend will show you DNS records to add (usually 3 records)
Step 2 — Add DNS records in GoDaddy
Add each record Resend gives you (TXT and MX records)
Click Verify DNS — usually takes 5-10 minutes
Change sender email from onboarding@resend.dev → noreply@onestopimmigrationstation.com
Go to Sign In/Providers → turn Confirm email back ON
Save this conversation link so we can pick up exactly where we left off. See you tomorrow!
OK have everything in context in Memory.MD and keep handy the list of tasks in TASKA.MD for the To do tasks
Both files are already up to date and pushed to GitHub. Let me do a final check to confirm everything is saved:
Both files are current. Here's a quick summary of what's saved:
All credentials (Supabase keys, Google OAuth, Resend API key)
High priority tasks (domain, email, dashboard pages)
Step by step guide of everything done
